length_bias_check.verification_method: Every answer-line's char length (text before the '#' feedback delimiter) was hand-counted directly from the written quiz.gift in groups of 10 characters, independently of the number that was first drafted into this sidecar. This second pass caught and corrected a genuine arithmetic slip in Q1's original count (it had been mis-tallied as correct=95/distractors=[115,108,104]; the verified re-count is correct=94/distractors=[105,99,94]). All other questions' first-pass counts (Q2-Q11) were reproduced exactly on re-count and are unchanged.
length_bias_check.summary: All 11 MCQs land between 0.935x and 1.138x of their distractor mean -- well inside the +/-20% band and none trip the >1.20 flag rule. Correct answer is the single longest option in only 3/11 questions (q2, q4, q6), close to the 25% chance rate for a 4-option item, so length does not systematically mark the correct answer.
